title: MetalLB LoadBalancer Service Stuck in Pending / No External IP Assigned
symptoms: kubectl get svc shows EXTERNAL-IP as <pending> indefinitely
Services of type LoadBalancer never receive an external IP
metallb-speaker pod logs show 'no available IPs'
causes: IPAddressPool exhausted — all IPs in pool already assigned
L2Advertisement or BGPAdvertisement not matching the service namespace/selector
metallb-speaker pods not running on all nodes
IPAddressPool not created or has wrong CIDR
remediation: Check pool capacity: compare assigned IPs vs pool range
Expand IPAddressPool: edit the pool CR to add more IP ranges
If L2Advertisement missing selector, add namespace selector to match new services
Restart speaker daemonset: kubectl -n metallb-system rollout restart ds/speaker
Restart controller: kubectl -n metallb-system rollout restart deploy/controller
Verify ARP/NDP: on router/switch confirm MAC entries for pool IPs are present
escalation: Pool exhaustion requires network team coordination to expand the IP range 10.0.0.200-10.0.0.250.

title: MetalLB Speaker Pod CrashLoopBackOff
symptoms: kubectl -n metallb-system get pods shows speaker pods in CrashLoopBackOff
Existing LoadBalancer services losing external connectivity
ARP responses for VIPs stop working
causes: Invalid MetalLB configuration (malformed IPAddressPool or Advertisement CRs)
Permissions issue — speaker cannot bind to raw network sockets
Node kernel missing ARP/NDP support (rare on Flatcar)
remediation: Fix any malformed CRs: kubectl -n metallb-system edit ipaddresspool <name>
If config invalid: kubectl -n metallb-system delete ipaddresspool <bad> and recreate
Check SecurityContext: speaker needs NET_RAW capability — verify PSP/PSA allows it
Recreate speaker pods: kubectl -n metallb-system rollout restart ds/speaker
Verify connectivity restored: arping <vip> from an external host
escalation: If all speaker pods are down, all LoadBalancer VIPs go dark. This is P1 for external service access.

title: MetalLB IP Address Conflict with Existing Network
symptoms: External IP assigned but service unreachable from outside
Another host on the network has the same IP
causes: IPAddressPool CIDR overlaps with DHCP range or static assignments
Another MetalLB or keepalived instance claiming same IPs
Physical host or VM using an IP in the MetalLB pool range
investigation: kubectl -n metallb-system get ipaddresspool -o yaml  # verify range
# Check DHCP server exclusions include MetalLB range 10.0.0.200-10.0.0.250
# Ping sweep: nmap -sP 10.0.0.200-250 from external host
remediation: Identify the conflicting host and remove/reassign its IP
Update IPAddressPool to avoid conflicting range: kubectl -n metallb-system edit ipaddresspool
Coordinate with network team to reserve MetalLB pool in DHCP exclusions
Use gratuitous ARP to reclaim IPs: MetalLB will re-announce on speaker restart
escalation: IP conflicts require network team intervention to resolve at the DHCP/switch level.
